headline: ShinyHunters looted gigabytes from hundreds of organizations through an Oracle PeopleSoft zero-day, capping a day that turned on actively exploited enterprise software and AI-driven crime.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Weaponization
emerging_trends.text: Criminals and researchers bend AI both directions, from Gemini-powered smishing to Agentjacking and LangGraph flaws that turn AI agents themselves into targets.

emerging_trends.subject: Enterprise Edge Exploitation
emerging_trends.text: Live exploitation of PeopleSoft, Ivanti Sentry, and Check Point VPN shows attackers racing defenders to internet-facing enterprise software.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: Hijacked Arch AUR packages and dark-web sales of repository access mark developer code as a prime attacker foothold.

emerging_trends.subject: Law Enforcement Momentum
emerging_trends.text: Takedowns of AudiA6 and Sniper Dz alongside a Conti guilty plea squeeze cybercrime's money and infrastructure at once.

emerging_trends.subject: Costly Breaches
emerging_trends.text: Coupang's record $409 million fine and the 23andMe settlement fund push the price of data loss to new highs.

topics.title: Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day Ravages Universities
topics.last_24h: Google confirmed ShinyHunters exploited CVE-2026-35273, a missing-authentication flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft, and CISA added it to the known exploited vulnerabilities catalog.
topics.summary: The bug lets attackers reach a critical PeopleTools function with no authentication. Oracle has mitigated it, though the company has not publicly confirmed in-the-wild abuse. ShinyHunters used the access to steal gigabytes from hundreds of organizations, hitting American universities hardest. Administrators running PeopleSoft should apply Oracle's fix and hunt for data exfiltration now.

sources.title: PeopleSoft 0-day affecting hundreds of organizations steals gigabytes of data
ShinyHunters Uses Oracle Zero-Day to Rampage Higher Ed
Google Confirms Exploitation of Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day by ShinyHunters
CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog

topics.title: CISA Orders 3-Day Ivanti Sentry Patch
topics.last_24h: CISA's new Binding Operational Directive 26-04 gives federal agencies until Sunday to patch an actively exploited Ivanti Sentry command-injection flaw as honeypots log exploitation attempts.
topics.summary: The critical OS command-injection bug lets attackers run code with root privileges on Ivanti Sentry gateways. Researchers watching honeypots see attackers probing the flaw, a sign of broad opportunistic scanning. Federal agencies face a three-day deadline under BOD 26-04, and private operators should move on the same urgency.

sources.title: CISA orders feds to patch actively exploited Ivanti flaw by Sunday
Ivanti Sentry Exploitation Attempts Hitting Honeypots

topics.title: 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked
topics.last_24h: Attackers rewrote build scripts in more than 400 Arch User Repository packages this week to drop a Rust infostealer and an eBPF rootkit.
topics.summary: The AUR is Arch Linux's community package collection, where build scripts run on a user's own machine. The hijacked packages install a Rust binary that harvests developer credentials and access tokens, and with root it loads an eBPF rootkit to hide itself. Developers who built any AUR package this week should rotate secrets and inspect their systems for the rootkit.

sources.title: Over 400 Arch Linux AUR Packages Hijacked to Deploy Infostealer and eBPF Rootkit
Over 400 Arch Linux packages compromised to push rootkit, infostealer

topics.title: Police Hit Crypto Laundering and Phishing Networks
topics.last_24h: Europol dismantled AudiA6, a crypto-laundering service that washed over €336 million for ransomware gangs, as a Ukrainian pleaded guilty to Conti charges and INTERPOL's Operation Ramz felled the Sniper Dz phishing platform.
topics.summary: AudiA6 laundered more than €336 million (~$389 million) in ransomware and cybercrime proceeds between 2022 and 2025 before authorities seized it. INTERPOL's Operation Ramz arrested 201 people across 13 MENA countries and shut the decade-old Sniper Dz PhaaS platform. A Ukrainian national extradited from Ireland admitted conspiracy tied to Conti, extending the pressure on ransomware money flows.

sources.title: Authorities dismantle crypto laundering service that moved €336 million for cybercriminals
INTERPOL Operation Takes Down Sniper Dz Phishing Platform, Arrests Administrator
Ukrainian national pleads guilty to role in Conti ransomware operation

topics.title: Google Sues China-Based Gemini Smishing Network
topics.last_24h: Google sued Outsider Enterprise, a China-based network it accuses of weaponizing its Gemini AI to run a phishing-as-a-service kit that blasted scam texts at Americans.
topics.summary: The operation built the Outsider PhaaS kit and used Gemini to generate phishing sites and scam infrastructure, linked to more than 9,000 fake sites and a million fraudulent URLs. Google estimates hundreds of thousands of victims and millions of dollars in losses. The suit marks a push to wield civil litigation against criminals who industrialize fraud with AI.

sources.title: Google Sues Chinese Smishing Network Accused of Using Gemini AI in Phishing
Google sues China-based scammers over Gemini AI abuse

topics.title: New Attacks Target AI Coding Agents
topics.last_24h: Researchers detailed Agentjacking, which tricks AI coding agents into running arbitrary code through fake Sentry error reports, and a LangGraph vulnerability chain enabling remote code execution.
topics.summary: Tenet Security's Agentjacking abuses crafted Sentry error reports to push AI coding agents into executing attacker code on developer machines. Three now-patched LangGraph flaws, including an SQL injection, chain into remote code execution on self-hosted AI agents. The findings show autonomous agents widening the attack surface as they read documents, call APIs, and run code. Teams running self-hosted agents should patch LangGraph and constrain agent execution.

sources.title: Agentjacking Attack Tricks AI Coding Agents Into Running Malicious Code
LangGraph Flaw Chain Exposes Self-Hosted AI Agents to Remote Code Execution

topics.title: Velvet Ant Backdoored Linux Login for a Decade
topics.last_24h: Sygnia disclosed that the China-linked Velvet Ant group backdoored the PAM and OpenSSH components governing Linux logins, hiding for close to ten years.
topics.summary: Velvet Ant planted its access in the authentication layer that decides who may sign in, where routine cleanup could not reach. Sygnia tracks the group as a China-nexus actor built for long-term espionage. Defenders should audit PAM modules and OpenSSH binaries for tampering across critical infrastructure.

sources.title: China-Linked Hackers Backdoored Linux Login Software to Hide for Nearly a Decade

topics.title: Record Privacy Fines and Fresh Breaches Pile Up
topics.last_24h: South Korea fined Coupang a record $409 million over a data breach, a bankruptcy administrator approved a $47 million fund for 23andMe victims, and Novo Nordisk and France's Tchap messenger disclosed new breaches.
topics.summary: Coupang's $409 million penalty is the largest ever from the commission, topping a $88.8 million fine against SK Telecom earlier this year. The 23andMe fund compensates 7 million customers whose genetic data leaked starting April 2023, much of it posted to the dark web. Novo Nordisk, the world's largest insulin maker, exposed clinical-trial patient data, and a breach of France's Tchap encrypted messenger hit over 73,000 public-sector accounts. Regulators and courts keep raising the cost of weak data stewardship.

sources.title: South Korea hits Coupang with record $409 million fine over data breach
Bankruptcy admin approves settlement fund of $47 million for 23andMe data breach victims
Pharma giant Novo Nordisk discloses breach of clinical trials data
Over 73,000 French govt employees affected in Tchap messenger breach

sports: Ohtani homers, leaves game with left knee inflammation
Marlins bring 5-game win streak into matchup with the
2026 MLB ABS challenge system tracker: Team, player

business_politics.text: The United States and Iran agreed the text of a peace deal, Pakistan's prime minister said, with both sides expecting to sign within days; the accord would dismantle Iran's nuclear program in return for sanctions relief and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Oil settled at three-month lows and Treasury yields fell on the week as the energy shock eased.

sources.title: US-Iran peace deal text agreed, says Pakistan's PM
Oil touches three-month low after Trump says US close to Iran deal

business_politics.text: SpaceX jumped nearly a fifth on its trading debut to a $2.1 trillion valuation after raising $75 billion in the largest initial public offering on record, making Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire as investors bought into the rocket-and-AI vision.

sources.title: SpaceX's surge on debut makes Musk world's first trillionaire
SpaceX, Now Worth $2.1 Trillion, Pulls Off Goldilocks Debut

business.text: Pittsburgh's Urban Redevelopment Authority won a $250,000 grant to upgrade Smithfield Street downtown and has hired an architect to transform the boarded-up storefronts below the park.

sources.title: Pittsburgh receives $250K grant to use for upgrades to Smithfield Street

business.text: Pennsylvania's Public Utility Commission will hold three public hearings on the proposed $32.8 million sale of the water authority serving the Donegal area and several Fayette County communities to the state's largest private water utility.

sources.title: PUC to hold hearings on Donegal area water company sale

business.text: First National Bank closed its branch on Tarentum Bridge Road and referred customers to other locations.

sources.title: First National Bank along Tarentum Bridge Road closes its doors

around_town.text: Mister Rogers' Neighborhood arrived on YouTube with its first dedicated channel, offering full episodes, livestreams, and clips of the Pittsburgh-made program to viewers worldwide.

sources.title: The first-ever dedicated Mister Rogers' Neighborhood YouTube channel launches globally

around_town.text: Pittsburgh officials want to open a 110-year-old time capsule that Mayor Joseph Armstrong placed in the City-County Building in 1916, holding newspaper clippings, a Bible, and city and national flags; they cannot find it.

sources.title: Pittsburgh wants to open a time capsule hidden in the City-County Building. But no one can find it.

around_town.text: Monroeville's mayor says the timeline for demolishing Monroeville Mall remains uncertain, with no formal plans yet submitted.

sources.title: Monroeville Mall demolition timeline up in the air, according to mayor

around_town.text: South Greensburg will ask voters to shrink its borough council, part of officials' push for a smaller local government.

sources.title: South Greensburg asks voters to reduce size of council

events.text: The Three Rivers Arts Festival enters its final weekend at its new home, Arts Landing in the Cultural District, where vendors report a warm reception after Thursday's storms cleared.

sources.title: Vendors approve of new location of Three Rivers Arts Festival

events.text: Seton Hill University in Greensburg hosts the annual Jane Austen Summer Program, a four-day symposium of lectures, small-group discussions, and a formal ball.

sources.title: Seton Hill site of symposium on Jane Austen

events.text: 1846 Farms in Westmoreland County hosts the Luke Bryan Farm Tour, the property's first major concert.

sources.title: Westmoreland County farm making preps for Luke Bryan Farm Tour, its first-ever concert

events.text: Butler County, where the Bantam Jeep was designed, marks the vehicle's heritage with a weekend of Jeep gatherings for owners and enthusiasts.

sources.title: 'A family affair': Butler County residents celebrate history of Jeeps in their community

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Premium: The Silicon Valley Bubble (Part 1)
reading.summary: Zitron argues the AI era is ending as OpenAI and Anthropic both file to go public, racing for exit liquidity while burning billions a year with no path to profitability.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: Fable 5, Anthropic Alignment, AI Tiers
reading.summary: Reviews Anthropic's Fable 5, the public version of Mythos, judging it highly capable while warning that it sets troubling new precedents.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: The iPhone's Last Stand
reading.summary: Contends Apple's revamped Siri, short of state of the art, works well enough to satisfy the consumer market and carry the iPhone forward.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: Why Isn't AI Taking Our Jobs?
reading.summary: Newport asks why AI has yet to eliminate jobs despite industry leaders comparing it to industrial automation, probing the limits of the brawn-to-brains analogy.
